questions.question: Which event marked the pivot moment in the extremist and moderate struggle during the Indian freedom struggle?
What legal document allowed Princely States to join either India or Pakistan after independence?
How many small states of present-day Odisha were integrated into the Indian Union at the time of Partition?
Under the Government of India Act, 1919, which subjects were transferred to Indian ministers?
Which method best characterises the approach of the Moderates?
Which of the following movements is also known as the 'Ulgulan' or the 'Great Tumult' movement?
The ‘Kundara Proclamation’ which openly called for taking up arms against the British, is associated with which of the following freedom fighters?
Narayana Guru, a social reformer who led a massive movement against the discrimination of the lower castes, was from the state of:
Which of the following was the first state to be formed on linguistic basis after severe agitation and the death of a protester?
Which of the following Acts marked the beginning of British parliamentary control over the East India Company?
Where was Gandhi's method of Satyagraha first evolved and later fully developed as a freedom struggle tool in India?
Womesh Chandra Bonnerjee represented Calcutta University in the Bengal Legislative Council during _________.
During the colonial period in India, which social reformer worked for widow remarriage and against child marriage in Bengal?
The Nizam of Hyderabad accepted British subsidiary alliance under ________________.
Which 18ᵗʰ-century revolt was led by Raja Chait Singh against British interference in the Benares region in 1781?
Which Act created the post of the Governor-General of Bengal?
The Faraizi Movement, led by Haji Shariatullah, focused on religious purification and resisting the British in which region?
Who is known as the 'Grand Old Man of India' and promoted Indian economic nationalism?
In which of the following years was the Bengal Sati Regulation enacted?
The association of Devi Choudhurani and Bhawani Pathak with the Faqir-Sanyasi Rebellion in India indicates which of the following?
questions.option_1: The movement was dominated by upper-caste elites.
questions.option_2: Only tribal groups led such resistances in Bengal.
questions.option_3: The rebellion had cross-religious and gender participation.
questions.option_4: The rebellions had a purely religious character.

questions.question: Who founded the Servants of India Society to promote national education and social reform in India?
When was the Bank of Madras established as the third Presidency Bank in India?
What was the main belief of the moderate Congress leaders regarding the British rule in India?
Which Act first allowed Indian members to discuss the budget in legislative councils?
The Revolt of 1857 created which of the following negative consequences in Indian society?
questions.option_1: Weakening of tribal and peasants’ movements
questions.option_2: Decline of Indian languages and culture
questions.option_4: Distrust between Hindu and Muslim communities
